analysis.test: Five-year statute of limitations — art. 2224 C. civ. (5 years running from the date on which the holder knew or should have known the relevant facts) + standing — a vested, present, and legitimate interest (art. 31 CPC) + personal, direct, and certain injury (art. 1240 C. civ.). Cassiopée must demonstrate (i) that its claim is not time-barred and (ii) that it can establish a distinct injury of its own, separate from that alleged in the infringement cause of action.
faits: Méridian Studios has publicly exploited Bastille since at least July 2018 (order […] 2, Exhibit 7), with trademark registration no. 4449595 (Exhibit 2) and Kbis (Exhibit 1) — a well-known and continuous exploitation, visible to any reasonably vigilant competitor in the board-game industry.

The Amazon screenshot dated January 20, 2020 (Exhibit 6 Bis) and the bailiff's reports of October 2019 (Exhibits 5, 8) establish a public exploitation enforceable against any normally diligent competitor.

The TJ Paris (Exhibit 12, December 18, 2020) has already ruled in proceedings brought by Cassiopée against Méridian Studios: Cassiopée had knowledge of the disputed facts no later than that date — the limitations period begins to run no later than December 2020, and potentially as early as July 2018.

Cassiopée did not send its mise en demeure (cease-and-desist letter) until September 2021 (letter of September 13, 2021), more than three years after the commencement of the public exploitation of Bastille.

No exhibit filed by Cassiopée demonstrates a personal, direct, and certain injury suffered by Cassiopée as a result of Méridian Studios' exploitation of Bastille (Exhibit 5 — Méridian Studios' letter of October 5, 2021 expressly contests this).

Méridian Studios has exploited Bastille under its own name in a peaceful and unequivocal manner since 2018 (Exhibits 1, 2, 7), which […] confers upon it the presumption of ownership as against third parties.

The Quiz Mania exploited by Mr. Camille Lefèvre constitutes a separate and subsequent act, with its own limitations period — but Cassiopée must establish that it had specific knowledge of that exploitation for its claim on that basis to be admissible.
analysis.faille: Cassiopée may argue that each act of exploitation of Tohu-Bohu by Julien Mercier constitutes an independent and continuing tortious act, thereby resetting the accrual date of the statute of limitations with respect to that specific exploitation. Furthermore, should Cassiopée assert standing as an original author (rather than as an assignee), the […] question of ownership of the rights in Bastille remains to be adjudicated, which […] could affect the admissibility of its claims.
analysis.riposte: Argue that (i) the statute of limitations runs from the date of actual knowledge of the facts, not from the date of their cessation — decisions [1] and [2] expressly confirm this; (ii) Cassiopée, an established player in the board-game industry, cannot seriously claim to have been unaware of the existence of Tohu-Bohu if that application was publicly accessible; (iii) as to standing, Méridian Studios benefits from the presumption of ownership arising from its open and notorious exploitation under its own name since 2018 (Exhibits 1, 2, 7), a presumption that Cassiopée must rebut by affirmative proof of its own title.
analysis.pourquoi: The combination of Exhibit 7 (July 2018), Exhibit 2 (registered trademark), Exhibit 6 Bis (Amazon, January 2020), and Exhibit 12 (judgment, December 2020) establishes a […] tight chain of evidence demonstrating that Cassiopée had, or should have had, knowledge of the facts well before any action was taken. Case law [1] and [2] confirms that the statute of limitations begins to run from the date of actual or constructive knowledge, without waiting for a mise en demeure (cease-and-desist letter).

autorites.title: Cass. civ. 1, No. 23-18.669, Sept. 3, 2025
autorites.summary:
Aux termes de l'article 2224 du code civil, les actions personnelles ou mobilières se prescrivent par cinq ans à compter du jour où le titulaire d'un droit a connu ou aurait dû connaître les faits lui permettant de l'exercer.
Il s'en déduit que lorsque la contrefaçon résulte d'une succession d'actes distincts, qu'il s'agisse d'actes de reproduction, de représentation ou de diffusion, et non d'un acte unique de cette nature s'étant prolongé dans le temps, la prescription court pour chacun de ces actes, à compter du jour où l'auteur a connu un tel acte ou aurait dû en avoir connaissance

autorites.title: Cass. civ. 1, No. 22-23.266, Nov. 15, 2023
autorites.summary:
Aux termes de l'article 2224 du code civil, les actions personnelles ou mobilières se prescrivent par cinq ans à compter du jour où le titulaire d'un droit a connu ou aurait dû connaître les faits lui permettant de l'exercer.
C'est, dès lors, à bon droit qu'une cour d'appel retient que le délai de prescription d'une action fondée sur la contrefaçon a commencé à courir à la date à laquelle avait été admis le caractère contrefaisant  d'une oeuvre, même si la contrefaçon s'inscrivait dans la durée

analysis.acte_referme: Bailiff's report (constat d'huissier) bearing a certified date establishing the […] initial public posting of Tohu-Bohu and the date on which Cassiopée acquired knowledge thereof, […] in support of the showing that Cassiopée was aware of the exploitation of Bastille as early as 2018–2019 (through Exhibits 7, 6 Bis, and 12).
analysis.moyen_adverse: Cassiopée contends that the exploitation of Tohu-Bohu by Julien Mercier constitutes a new and continuing act of infringement of which it only recently became aware, so that the five-year statute of limitations has not run with respect to that specific exploitation. It also invokes its status as original author as the basis for its standing to sue.
analysis.these_adverse: Cassiopée argues, in light of the judgment of the Tribunal judiciaire de Paris dated December 18, 2020 (Exhibit 12) and the judgment of the Cour d'appel d'Aix-en-Provence dated June 21, 2018 (Exhibit 16), that it holds the copyright in the original game from which Bastille is allegedly derived, and that this status confers upon it a legitimate and current interest in bringing an action against any unauthorized exploitation, including through Tohu-Bohu. It maintains that each act of exploitation of Tohu-Bohu constitutes an independent triggering event whose limitation period has not expired, and that the cease-and-desist letter (mise en demeure) of September 13, 2021 in any event interrupted the statute of limitations with respect to acts occurring thereafter.

pieces_adverses.piece: Agreement or document establishing Cassiopée's ownership of the original game predating Bastille
pieces_adverses.contre: Challenge the certain date [date certaine] and enforceability against third parties; verify whether the assignment actually covers the rights claimed in Bastille

pieces_adverses.piece: Screenshot or process-server's report [constat d'huissier] establishing the date on which Cassiopée discovered Tohu-Bohu, for purposes of fixing the starting point of the statute of limitations
pieces_adverses.contre: Counter that the presumed knowledge of a vigilant competitor predates the alleged date, in light of the public availability of the application

pieces_adverses.piece: Judgment of the Tribunal judiciaire de Paris dated December 18, 2020 (Exhibit 12) invoked as proof of Cassiopée's ownership
pieces_adverses.contre: That judgment rules on prior facts and does not definitively resolve ownership of Bastille as exploited by Méridian Studios since 2018; its res judicata effect is […] to the subject matter of the present proceedings.

pieces_attendues: Bailiff's report (constat d'huissier) establishing the date of […] initial public posting of Quiz Mania and its visibility to any competitor in the industry.
Kbis extract and exploitation history of Méridian Studios demonstrating the priority and continuity of the exploitation of Bastille since 2018
Comprehensive […] record of prior judicial proceedings (in particular TJ Paris, December 18, 2020) establishing that Cassiopée had knowledge of the facts at issue prior to 2021.
Certified public accountant's attestation or Bastille sales records establishing the absence of any loss attributable to Cassiopée
